title: Tuscan Cheese Potato Bake
ingredients: 1.5 tsp snipped fresh thyme or ½ tsp dried thyme, crushed
4 oz Parmesan cheese, finely shredded (1 C)
½ C panko (Japanese style bread crumbs)
¼ tsp dried Italian seasoning, crushed
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Lightly grease a 2 qt. square baking dish and set aside.
Scrub potatoes and cut into 1 inch pieces.
In a large saucepan, cook potatoes in lightly salted boiling water for 12-15 minutes or until tender, then drain.
In a 12 inch skillet, cook and stir garlic and thyme in butter over medium heat for 1 minute.
Add potatoes to the skillet and coarsely mash them.
Stir in buttermilk, ½ tsp salt, and ¼ tsp black pepper.
Fold in fontina cheese, half of the Parmesan cheese, and the blue cheese.
Evenly spread the potato mixture in the prepared baking dish.
In a small bowl, combine remaining Parmesan, panko, Italian seasoning, and oil, tossing with a fork to combine.
Evenly sprinkle the panko mixture over the potato mixture in the dish.
Bake for 20 minutes or until bubbly and the top is golden.
Sprinkle with fresh snipped parsley before serving.
